description_long: This object is a vintage manual coffee grinder, featuring a square wooden base with a small pull-out drawer for collecting ground coffee. The top section is fitted with a large, round metal grinding mechanism, including a prominent hand crank with a knob for manual operation. The wood appears aged and slightly worn, with visible grain and a warm brown finish, while the metal parts are dark, possibly cast iron, and have a matte appearance. The drawer is partially open, revealing a dark interior likely used for collecting coffee grounds. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, with visible screws and a sturdy construction. There are no visible brand markings or modern electronic components, emphasizing its traditional, mechanical nature.
description: A vintage manual coffee grinder with a wooden base, metal hand crank, and a pull-out drawer for collecting grounds. The design is robust and traditional, with aged wood and dark metal components.
description_view_0: The coffee grinder is seen from a front angle, highlighting the open drawer and the full length of the hand crank extending to the left.
description_view_1: This view shows the grinder from a slightly rotated front-left perspective, emphasizing the open drawer and the circular grinding mechanism on top.
description_view_2: From the back-left, the hand crank is visible extending outward, and the side of the wooden base and partially open drawer are shown.
description_view_3: The rear view focuses on the back and top of the grinder, with the hand crank and grinding mechanism most prominent, and the drawer less visible.
